_fixture_note: Real output from 2026-07-16 against RAW.RAW_ORDER_ITEMS (20,000 rows, ~1MB). This is the actual second live run, after fixing a real bug the first run found: SYSTEM$CLUSTERING_INFORMATION errors on a table with no explicit clustering key ('table X is not clustered') rather than returning natural-organization stats -- the script now catches that specific message and reports clustered: false cleanly, with an empty errors object, instead of surfacing it as a data-access failure.
heuristic_note: Candidate columns are found via text-matching real column names against the non-SELECT-list portion of poorly-pruned queries -- not a SQL parser. It can miss a real filter written unusually, and can't fully rule out a same-named column from a different, joined table.

advisory.detail: Table is 0.001 GB, below the 1.0 GB threshold this check uses -- clustering overhead (reclustering credits) isn't worth it at this size regardless of query patterns.
